prompt: The null-safe sort throws NullPointerException. What is the bug?
choices: Comparator.naturalOrder() sorts in descending order by default
Comparator.naturalOrder() calls compareTo on each element; comparing null to a String throws NullPointerException; use Comparator.nullsFirst(Comparator.naturalOrder()) to handle nulls
Lists containing null cannot be sorted in Java
naturalOrder() requires all elements to implement Comparable explicitly
explanation: naturalOrder() delegates to element.compareTo(), which throws NullPointerException when called on or with null. Fix: Comparator.nullsFirst(Comparator.naturalOrder()) places nulls at the front, or Comparator.nullsLast(Comparator.naturalOrder()) places them at the back.
